content:
# John Chapter 7 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/JHN_007_page_001.jpg, JHN_007_page_002.jpg, JHN_007_page_003.jpg

1. And after these things the Yeshua was walking in the Galilaia; for he did not wish to walk in the Ioudaia, because the Ioudaioi were seeking to kill him.

2. And the feast of the Ioudaioi was near, the Tabernacles.

3. Then the brothers of him said to him, "Depart from here and go into the Ioudaia, that also the disciples of you may see the works of you that you do.

4. For no one does anything in secret and himself seeks to be in openness. If you do these things, show yourself to the world."

5. For neither the brothers of him were believing in him.

6. Then the Yeshua says to them, "The time of me is not yet present, but the time of you is always ready.

7. The world is not able to hate you, but me it hates, because I testify concerning it that the works of it are evil.

8. You go up to the feast. I am not going up to this feast, for the time of me has not yet been fulfilled."

9. And having said these things, he remained in the Galilaia.

10. But when the brothers of him went up to the feast, then he also went up, not openly but as in secret.

11. Then the Ioudaioi were seeking him at the feast and saying, "Where is that [one]?"

12. And there was much grumbling concerning him among the crowds. Some said, "He is good." Others said, "No, but he deceives the crowd."

13. Yet no one was speaking openly concerning him because of the fear of the Ioudaioi.

14. And already [the] feast being in [the] middle, the Yeshua went up into the temple and was teaching.

15. Then the Ioudaioi marveled, saying, "How does this [one] know letters, not having studied?"

16. Then the Yeshua answered them and said, "The teaching of me is not mine but [belongs] to the [one] having sent me.

17. If anyone wills to do the will of him, he will know concerning the teaching, whether it is from the God or I speak from myself.

18. The [one] speaking from himself seeks the own glory of him; but the [one] seeking the glory of the [one] having sent him, this [one] is true, and unrighteousness is not in him.

19. Did Mouses not give you the law? And none of you does the law. Why do you seek to kill me?"

20. The crowd answered, "You have a demon. Who seeks to kill you?"

21. The Yeshua answered and said to them, "One work I did, and all of you marvel.

22. Because of this Mouses has given to you the circumcision — not that it is from Mouses but from the fathers — and on [the] Sabbath you circumcise a man.

23. If a man receives circumcision on [the] Sabbath that the law of Mouses may not be broken, are you angry with me because I made a whole man well on [the] Sabbath?

24. Do not judge according to appearance, but judge the righteous judgment."

25. Then some of the [ones] of Hierosolyma said, "Is this not the [one] whom they seek to kill?

26. And behold, he speaks openly, and they say nothing to him. Did the rulers truly know that this is the Christos?

27. But we know this [one], from where he is. But the Christos, when he comes, no one knows from where he is."

28. Then the Yeshua cried out in the temple, teaching and saying, "You both know me and know from where I am, and I have not come from myself, but the [one] having sent me is true, whom you do not know.

29. I know him, because from him I am, and that [one] sent me."

30. Then they were seeking to seize him, and no one laid the hand upon him, because the hour of him had not yet come.

31. But many of the crowd believed in him and said, "The Christos, when he comes, will he do more signs than what this [one] did?"

32. The Pharisaioi heard the crowd grumbling these things concerning him, and the chief priests and the Pharisaioi sent attendants that they might seize him.

33. Then the Yeshua said, "Still a little time I am with you, and I go to the [one] having sent me.

34. You will seek me and will not find [me], and where I am, you are not able to come."

35. Then the Ioudaioi said to one another, "Where is this [one] about to go that we will not find him? Is he about to go to the dispersion of the Greeks and teach the Greeks?

36. What is this word that he said, 'You will seek me and will not find [me], and where I am, you are not able to come'?"

37. And on the last day, the great [day] of the feast, the Yeshua stood and cried out, saying, "If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink.

38. The [one] believing in me, just as the scripture said, rivers of living water will flow from the belly of him."

39. But this he said concerning the Spirit, which the [ones] believing in him were about to receive; for [the] Spirit was not yet, because the Yeshua was not yet glorified.

40. [Some] of the crowd therefore, having heard these words, said, "This is truly the Prophet."

41. Others said, "This is the Christos." But others said, "For does the Christos come from the Galilaia?

42. Did the scripture not say that from the seed of David and from Bethlehem, the village where David was, the Christos comes?"

43. Then a division happened in the crowd because of him.

44. And some of them wished to seize him, but no one laid the hands upon him.

45. Then the attendants came to the chief priests and Pharisaioi, and those said to them, "Why did you not bring him?"

46. The attendants answered, "Never did a man speak thus."

47. Then the Pharisaioi answered them, "Have you also been deceived?

48. Has anyone of the rulers believed in him, or of the Pharisaioi?

49. But this crowd, the [one] not knowing the law — accursed they are."

50. Nikodemos says to them — the [one] having come to him before, being one of them —

51. "Does the law of us judge the man unless it first hears from him and knows what he does?"

52. They answered and said to him, "Are you also from the Galilaia? Search and see that from the Galilaia a prophet does not arise."

[7:53-8:11 — The Pericope Adulterae (the woman caught in adultery) is not present in the Sinaiticus manuscript.]

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.8 — "I am not going up":** Sinaiticus reads οὐκ ("not") rather than οὔπω ("not yet") found in some manuscripts. The harder reading "not" (seemingly contradicted by v.10) is more likely original — later scribes changed it to "not yet" to resolve the apparent contradiction. Jesus changes his plan, or his "not going" refers to going publicly.

2. **v.37-38 — Punctuation ambiguity:** The Greek has no punctuation marks. The sentence can be divided two ways: (a) "If anyone thirsts, let him come to me, and let him drink who believes in me" — making the believer the source of rivers; or (b) "If anyone thirsts, let him come to me and drink. The one believing in me — as the scripture said — rivers will flow from his belly" — making the believer the channel. EMET follows option (b).

3. **v.39 — "the Spirit was not yet":** πνεῦμα γὰρ οὔπω ἦν — literally "the Spirit was not yet." Some later manuscripts add "given" (δεδομένον) to soften the statement. Sinaiticus has the bolder reading: the Spirit was "not yet" — meaning the Spirit's new-covenant role had not yet been inaugurated.

4. **v.52 — "a prophet does not arise from Galilee":** Sinaiticus reads the singular "a prophet" (προφήτης). Some manuscripts read "the prophet" (ὁ προφήτης). The Pharisees' claim is historically inaccurate — Jonah was from Gath-hepher in Galilee (2 Kings 14:25).

5. **7:53-8:11 — Absent:** The Pericope Adulterae (woman caught in adultery) is not in Sinaiticus, nor in any manuscript before the 5th century. It was likely an authentic oral tradition that was later inserted into John's Gospel at various points.

— EMET, AI Translator
